tldr.title: DeepSeek V4 Pro beats GPT-5.5 Pro on precision
tldr.author: yogthos
bullets: DeepSeek V4 Pro surpasses OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Pro in precision-critical evaluations, signaling a shift toward highly competitive open-architecture frontier models.
This precision milestone highlights the efficiency of DeepSeek's Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture and optimized Multi-Head Latent Attention (MLA) mechanisms.
AI engineers should benchmark V4 Pro for high-precision workflows including deterministic code generation, mathematics, and structured JSON parsing.

tldr.title: Making a game in Visual Studio from 1997
tldr.author: calvin
bullets: Developing in Visual Studio 97 (VC++ 5.0) requires managing pre-C++98 compiler limitations and legacy MSVC build systems on modern Windows.
Retro game loop design relies on legacy DirectX 5 or Win32 GDI, requiring manual memory management without modern STL or smart pointers.
Targeting Windows 95/98 requires handling 32-bit execution boundaries, legacy DLL dependencies, and static linking of old MSVCRT runtimes.

tldr.title: Dancing mad with sandboxing
tldr.author: cohix
bullets: Linux sandboxing relies on complex, error-prone combinations of namespaces, seccomp-bpf, and Landlock rather than a unified API.
OpenBSD's pledge and unveil offer simpler, safer sandboxing paradigms compared to the fragmented and low-level Linux primitives.
Implementing custom Linux sandboxes often introduces security risks due to subtle interactions between CLONE_NEWUSER and system privileges.

tldr.title: IPv6 zones in URLs are a mistake
tldr.author: gmem
bullets: IPv6 link-local zone identifiers (like %eth0) are host-specific, breaking portability when shared in distributed configuration files across different environments.
RFC 6874 defines IPv6 zone identifiers in URIs, but inconsistent browser and HTTP client parser implementations cause silent connection failures.
DevOps teams should avoid using IPv6 link-local addresses in configs; use global unicast addresses (GUA) or unique local addresses (ULA) instead.

tldr.title: RIP Anthony Head: Our 10 favorite moments of Buffy's Giles
tldr.author: Jennifer Ouellette
bullets: Ars Technica published a retrospective analyzing Anthony Head's performance as Rupert Giles in the Buffy the Vampire Slayer franchise.
The analysis highlights ten key scenes, focusing on how Head's acting anchored the ensemble cast and facilitated narrative progression.
The profile emphasizes the technique of 'quietly filling in the gaps,' demonstrating the high value of subtle, supporting execution in complex productions.
